Navigation

Pricing & Resources

Pricing

[Get Started](https://docs.moralis.com/) [Data API](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/overview) [Streams](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview) [Datashare](https://docs.moralis.com/datashare/overview) [Data Indexer](https://docs.moralis.com/data-indexer/overview) [RPC Nodes](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/overview) [Auth API](https://docs.moralis.com/auth-api/overview) [Changelog](https://docs.moralis.com/changelog)

On this page

- [What is a Compute Unit?](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#what-is-a-compute-unit)
- [CU Cost](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#cu-cost)
- [Dynamic Endpoints](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#dynamic-endpoints)
- [CU Throughput (CU/s)](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#cu-throughput-cu%2Fs)

All Moralis plans come with generous monthly request limits. The number of included requests depends on your plan - check the [pricing page](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#) for details.Moralis uses **Compute Units (CUs)** as a standard way to measure and bill for usage across all products - including [Data API](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/overview), [Streams](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview), [Datashare](https://docs.moralis.com/datashare/overview), and [RPC Nodes](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/overview).CUs provide a unified way to account for **computational complexity** and **rate limits**, regardless of the product youâ€™re using.

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing\#what-is-a-compute-unit)  What is a Compute Unit?

A **Compute Unit (CU)** represents the relative computational cost of a request, task, or operation within the Moralis platform. Simple operations consume fewer CUs, while more complex or resource-intensive ones consume more.For example:

- A basic wallet balance check may consume a small number of CUs.
- A multi-chain portfolio aggregation, or a high-frequency stream of on-chain events, may consume significantly more.

CUs ensure youâ€™re only billed for the resources you actually use - and that high-load workloads are fairly metered across the system.

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing\#cu-cost)  CU Cost

Each product applies CU-based billing differently. Generally:

- **CUs are deducted from your planâ€™s included quota**.
- **Usage beyond your quota may incur overage charges**, depending on your plan and product.

Visit the individual pricing pages to see how CUs apply to each product:

- [Data API Pricing](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/pricing)
- [Streams Pricing](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing)
- [Datashare Pricing](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#)
- [Nodes Pricing](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing)

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing\#dynamic-endpoints)  Dynamic Endpoints

Some API endpoints have **dynamic CU costs** that scale based on the scope of the request. Instead of a fixed cost per call, these endpoints charge a base CU amount multiplied by a variable factor such as the number of chains, wallets, or addresses involved.For example:

- An endpoint costing **50 CUs per chain** that queries 3 chains will consume **150 CUs** total.
- An endpoint costing **250 CUs per chain** that queries 5 chains will consume **1,250 CUs** total.

Dynamic endpoints are clearly marked on their respective documentation pages. You can find CU costs for all endpoints on the individual product pricing pages listed above.

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing\#cu-throughput-cu/s)  CU Throughput (CU/s)

CUs are also used to enforce **rate limits**, measured in **CUs per second (CU/s)**.This helps manage request throughput and protect system performance - allowing heavier requests to be made less frequently, while lightweight calls remain fast and responsive.Different products may enforce CU/s limits differently. Check the respective documentation for details.

[Quickstart Guides](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/quickstart-guides) [Supported Chains](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/supported-chains)

web.title: Pricing - Moralis
web.description: A Compute Unit is a measure of computational load assigned to each RPC request. Each method has a CU Weight that is deducted from your total available CUs.

[Moralis home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/moralis/GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO/logo/light.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO&q=85&s=66e6da0d81943c4679e0ba921850201d)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/moralis/GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO/logo/dark.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO&q=85&s=39f72ccb7cd088ee041f49d8b76acc8c)](https://docs.moralis.com/)

Navigation

Introduction

Pricing

[Get Started](https://docs.moralis.com/) [Data API](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/overview) [Streams](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview) [Datashare](https://docs.moralis.com/datashare/overview) [Data Indexer](https://docs.moralis.com/data-indexer/overview) [RPC Nodes](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/overview) [Auth API](https://docs.moralis.com/auth-api/overview) [Changelog](https://docs.moralis.com/changelog)

On this page

- [What is a Compute Unit (CU)?](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#what-is-a-compute-unit-cu-)
- [Plan Tiers](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#plan-tiers)
- [Standard RPC Method Costs](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#standard-rpc-method-costs)
- [Extended RPC Method Costs](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#extended-rpc-method-costs)
- [Archive Nodes](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#archive-nodes)
- [Optimizing CU Usage](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#optimizing-cu-usage)
- [Need Higher Limits?](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#need-higher-limits)

All [Moralis plans](https://moralis.io/pricing) include generous monthly limits on the number of requests you can make. Some requests are more computationally expensive than others, so heavier requests are assigned higher **Compute Unit (CU) weights**.

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#what-is-a-compute-unit-cu-)  What is a Compute Unit (CU)?

A **Compute Unit** is a measure of computational load assigned to each RPC request. Each method has a **CU Weight** that is deducted from your total available CUs. Archive requests (queries to historical data) often have a higher **Archive CU Weight**.

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#plan-tiers)  Plan Tiers

|  | Starter | Pro | Business | Enterprise |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CUs Included | 10,000,000 | 100,000,000 | 350,000,000 | Custom |
| API Throughput (CUs/second) | 1,500 | 1,500 | 3,000 | Custom |
| Node Throughput (CUs/second) | 100 | 100 | 300 | Custom |
| Number of Nodes | 2 | 5 | Unlimited | Unlimited |
| Archive Requests | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#standard-rpc-method-costs)  Standard RPC Method Costs

| RPC Method | CU Weight | Archive CU Weight |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `eth_blockNumber` | 3 | 3 |
| `eth_call` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_chainId` | 3 | 3 |
| `eth_createAccessList` | 3 | 3 |
| `eth_estimateGas` | 5 | 5 |
| `eth_feeHistory` | 3 | 3 |
| `eth_gasPrice` | 3 | 3 |
| `eth_getBalance` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getBlockByHash` | 12 | 12 |
| `eth_getBlockByNumber` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getBlockTransactionCountByHash` | 12 | 12 |
| `eth_getBlockTransactionCountByNumber` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getBlockReceipts` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getCode` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getLogs` | 12 | 12 |
| `eth_getProof` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getStorageAt` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getTransactionByHash` | 12 | 12 |
| `eth_getTransactionByBlockHashAndIndex` | 12 | 12 |
| `eth_getTransactionByBlockNumberAndIndex` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getTransactionCount` | 2 | 8 |
| `eth_getTransactionReceipt` | 8 | 8 |
| `eth_getUncleByBlockHashAndIndex` | 8 | 8 |
| `eth_getUncleByBlockNumberAndIndex` | 2 | 8 |
| `eth_getUncleCountByBlockHash` | 8 | 8 |
| `eth_getUncleCountByBlockNumber` | 2 | 8 |
| `eth_maxPriorityFeePerGas` | 2 | 2 |
| `eth_sendRawTransaction` | 3 | 3 |

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#extended-rpc-method-costs)  Extended RPC Method Costs

Moralis provides extended RPC methods that offer advanced data retrieval. These are more computationally intensive and have higher CU weights.

| Method | Description | CU Weight |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `eth_getTransactions` | Get native transactions by wallet address | 15 |
| `eth_getDecodedTransactions` | Get decoded wallet history | 30 |
| `eth_getTokenBalances` | Get ERC20 token balances by wallet | 25 |
| `eth_getTokenPrice` | Get ERC20 token price by token address | 25 |
| `eth_getTokenMetadata` | Get ERC20 token metadata by token address | 8 |
| `eth_getNFTBalances` | Get NFTs by wallet address | 20 |
| `eth_getNFTCollections` | Get NFT collections by wallet address | 20 |

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#archive-nodes)  Archive Nodes

Archive nodes store historical blockchain data beyond the most recent blocks. Because of the additional computational and storage resources required, querying archive nodes comes with a higher CU cost. If your use case involves accessing deep historical data, be mindful of the increased cost shown in the **Archive CU Weight** column above.

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#optimizing-cu-usage)  Optimizing CU Usage

- **Limit block range** â€” The default block range for queries is 100 blocks. Requesting larger ranges or querying archive data increases CU consumption.
- **Monitor usage** â€” Track your CU usage in the [Moralis Dashboard](https://admin.moralis.com/) to avoid hitting rate limits.
- **Use batch requests** â€” Combine multiple requests into a single API call. Note that batching does not reduce total CU cost, but it reduces HTTP overhead. Maximum batch size is 20 requests per call. See [Batch Requests](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-batch-requests) for details.

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#need-higher-limits)  Need Higher Limits?

If your use case requires more CUs than your current plan offers, consider upgrading or reaching out to the Moralis team for custom solutions at [moralis.io/pricing](https://moralis.io/pricing).

[Overview](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/overview) [Supported Chains](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/supported-chains)

Introduction

Overview

[Get Started](https://docs.moralis.com/) [Data API](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/overview) [Streams](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview) [Datashare](https://docs.moralis.com/datashare/overview) [Data Indexer](https://docs.moralis.com/data-indexer/overview) [RPC Nodes](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/overview) [Auth API](https://docs.moralis.com/auth-api/overview) [Changelog](https://docs.moralis.com/changelog)

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/\#moralis-data-platform)  Moralis Data Platform

Moralis is an onchain data platform that provides a complete, production-grade data layer for Web3.It enables teams to fetch, monitor, index, and export blockchain data across 30+ supported chains - without running RPCs, DIY indexers, or custom infrastructure.The platform is built for scale, low latency, and reliability, and is used by both startups and enterprises to power wallets, analytics, automation, and data pipelines.

[**Data APIs** \\
\\
Fetch Crypto Data](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/overview)

[**Streams** \\
\\
Monitor Crypto Data](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview)

[**Datashare** \\
\\
Export Crypto Data](https://docs.moralis.com/datashare/overview)

[**Data Indexer** \\
\\
Index Crypto Data](https://docs.moralis.com/data-indexer/overview)

### [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/\#data-api)  Data API

The [Data API](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/overview) provides a **single**, **consistent interface** for accessing decoded and enriched onchain data across EVM and Solana chains.It delivers **low-latency**, **high-throughput APIs** for wallets, tokens, NFTs, prices, DeFi, and raw blockchain data, allowing teams to build multi-chain applications from a single backend.**Best for:** powering user-facing applications, dashboards, and backend services that need fast, reliable onchain reads.

### [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/\#streams)  Streams

[Streams](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview) enables **real-time access to onchain events** through webhooks, allowing applications to react instantly without polling the blockchain.Teams can subscribe to wallet activity, transfers, contract events, or custom triggers and receive events as they happen onchain.**Best for:** notifications, automation, monitoring, and event-driven workflows.

### [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/\#datashare)  Datashare

[Datashare](https://docs.moralis.com/datashare/overview) provides access to **large-scale historical and ongoing onchain datasets**, delivered directly into cloud storage or data warehouses.It is designed for analytics, machine learning, compliance, and reporting workflows, exporting **normalized**, **decoded**, **cross-chain data** in formats optimized for data pipelines.**Best for:** analytics, BI, AI/ML training, compliance, and batch data processing.

### [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/\#data-indexer)  Data Indexer

The [Data Indexer](https://docs.moralis.com/data-indexer/overview) is a managed, enterprise-grade indexing solution for building **custom onchain data pipelines**.It combines historical backfills with real-time indexing, supports **custom schemas**, and delivers indexed data directly into your infrastructure - giving teams full control without the operational burden of running indexers in-house.**Best for:** custom data models, internal analytics systems, and enterprise workloads requiring tailored onchain data.

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/\#how-the-platform-fits-together)  How the platform fits together

- **Data API â†’** Read onchain data on demand with low latency
- **Streams â†’** React to onchain events in real time
- **Datashare â†’** Export and analyze onchain data at scale
- **Data Indexer â†’** Build custom, controlled onchain data pipelines

Together, these products form Moralisâ€™ **onchain data layer**, enabling teams to build, scale, and operate Web3 products on a single platform.

## [â€‹](https://docs.moralis.com/\#product-comparison)  Product comparison

| Product | Primary purpose | Access pattern | Data type | Best suited for |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Data API](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/overview) | Read onchain data | On-demand (request/response) | Decoded, enriched blockchain data | Apps, dashboards, backend services |
| [Streams](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview) | React to onchain events | Real-time webhooks | Live wallet & contract events | Notifications, automation, monitoring |
| [Datashare](https://docs.moralis.com/datashare/overview) | Analyze data at scale | Batch & scheduled exports | Large-scale historical datasets | Analytics, BI, AI/ML, compliance |
| [Data Indexer](https://docs.moralis.com/data-indexer/overview) | Build custom data pipelines | Continuous indexing | Custom-structured onchain data | Enterprise systems, internal analytics |

[Get Your API Key](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/get-your-api-key)
